_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 3-2 Stroke or Suspected Stroke (Revised 12/02/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Treatment Protocol Section 3-2: Stroke or Suspected Stroke — MDHHS Approved 12/2/22, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: Stroke or Suspected Stroke
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 3-2: Stroke or Suspected Stroke
points: Measure blood glucose (may be MFR skill — see Blood Glucose Testing-Procedure Protocol); if blood glucose is less than 60 mg/dL, treat per Altered Mental Status-Treatment Protocol

Utilize the Cincinnati Pre-hospital Stroke Scale (CPSS) or other MCA-approved stroke scale (including large vessel occlusion detection); see MCA stroke supplement if applicable — assessment must include at minimum: (A) Facial droop — have patient show teeth or smile; (B) Arm drift — have patient close eyes and hold both arms out for 10 seconds; (C) Speech abnormality — have patient say 'the sky is blue in Michigan'; (D) Time of last known well — determine and document; (E) Any deficit in a validated stroke scale is considered positive for stroke
Follow MCA Transport Protocol for facility selection and early alerting requirements
Contact destination hospital as soon as possible and begin transport
If available, encourage a family member to either accompany the patient or go to the receiving facility as soon as possible
Initiate vascular access — DO NOT delay scene time for IV; preferentially with 18 gauge (20 gauge minimally) (Specialist skill)
Monitor ECG — DO NOT delay scene time for ECG monitoring (Specialist skill)
See MCA stroke supplement if applicable

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may designate blood glucose testing as an MFR skill

MCA must select approved stroke scale(s) — must include at minimum Cincinnati Pre-hospital Stroke Scale (CPSS); MCAs using large vessel occlusion (LVO) detection scales must document in MCA stroke supplement
options: CPSS + LVO detection scale (see MCA stroke supplement)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must define stroke receiving facility hierarchy and early alerting requirements in MCA Transport Protocol and stroke supplement

deterioration_flags: Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL — treat per Altered Mental Status protocol before assigning stroke diagnosis
Seizure activity — treat per Seizure-Treatment Protocol; seizure may indicate hemorrhagic stroke or post-ictal state
Declining GCS — airway management; expedite transport; contact Medical Control
scope_notes: Stroke scale assessment, time of last known well documentation: all levels
Blood glucose testing: all levels — may be MFR skill per MCA selection
IV access (preferred 18 gauge): Specialist skill — DO NOT delay scene time
ECG monitoring: Specialist skill — DO NOT delay scene time
Transport destination: per MCA Transport Protocol and stroke supplement
